heading: Gratuities for survivors of deceased House employees; computation
label: 4553 Gratuities for survivors of deceased House employees; computation
text: The Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives is on and after , authorized to pay, from the applicable accounts of the House of Representatives, a gratuity to the widow, widower, or heirs-at-law, of each deceased employee of the House an amount equal to one month’s salary for each year or part of year of the first six years service of such employee plus one-half of one month’s salary for each year or part of year of such service in excess of six years to and including the eighteenth year of such service. Service computed hereunder shall include all Federal civilian employment, and military service where such service interrupted Federal civilian employment.July 2, 1954
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 125 of this title

Amendments1996— substituted “Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives” for “Clerk of the House” and “applicable accounts of the House of Representatives” for “contingent fund of the House”.Pub. L. 104–186
